ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.qq reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4== See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also! == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg|270px]] | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, [[Tamaki]], [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]][[Category:狭田国生神社]][[ja:狭田国生神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: pages]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satakunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[ja:狭田国生神社]] [[en:Satakunari Shrine]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]]

{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[ja:狭田国生神社]] [[en:Satakunari Shrine]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]]

Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = [[January 17]] | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in [[Tamaki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satakunari Shrine]] [[ja:狭田国生神社]] [[en:Satakunari Shrine]] [[sn:atakunari Shrine]]
