revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = {{ill|January 17|en|January 17|WD=Q2264|lt=January 17}} | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.490278|N|136.637694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|狭田国生神社|version=77002057|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Satakunari Shrine (Q17225991)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|lt=Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|WD=Q17225991}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || — || {{coord|34.490344|136.637711|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181118 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225991}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225991]] [[ja:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|狭田国生神社|version=77002057|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Satakunari Shrine (Q17225991)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Satakunari Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Satakunari-jinja 02.jpg | location = 322 Ushikaube, Sata, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_Min = 29 | Latitude_Sec = 25.0 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude_Min = 38 | Longitude_Sec = 15.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto
Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto
Yamasue-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = {{ill|January 17|en|January 17|WD=Q2264|lt=January 17|1=January 17}} | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.490278|N|136.637694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Established = Before the 26th year of Emperor Suinin's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Name = Haiko-san | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = {{ill|January 17|en|January 17|WD=Q2264|lt=January 17|1=January 17}} | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] Yuda Procession ([[October 21]]) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.490278|N|136.637694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Satakunari Shrine''' (狭田国生神社, *Satakunari Jinja*Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58) is an [[auxiliary shrine]] (*sessha*) of [[Ise Grand Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, *Naikū*), located on the left bank of the Sotojida River in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Haiko-san."Kaneko (1984): p. 34 == Overview == The shrine is located at 322 Ushikaupe, Sada, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.204 It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town. Among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 26th. The name "Sata" is presumed to derive from its location, "Sada." The local nickname "Haiko" is thought to be a variation of the enshrined deity Hayakawahiko no Mikoto.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.205 In "Seyō Gorin Ikkyō," it is noted that "commonly referred to as Haiko Shrine, the area is also known as 'Samukawa Shrine.'"Kaneko (1984): p.35 It is believed to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The shrine grounds cover 1,656 square meters. The shrine building, constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden planks, faces south and is enclosed by a single layer of tamagaki (sacred fence). There is no offertory box. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: Hayakawahiko no Mikoto and Hayakawahime no Mikoto, gods of the Sotojōda River flowing nearby, and Yamazue no Mitama, the guardian deity of the land. The "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" already mentions the enshrinement of these three deities, with "Hayakawa" referring to the Sotojōda River. The parent deities of Hayakawahiko and Hayakawahime are enshrined at {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}. == History == According to the "Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki," the shrine was established when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto, upon crossing a stream with Amaterasu Ōmikami, received an offering of sacred rice paddies from Hayakawa Hiko no Mikoto, leading to the shrine's foundation as "Hayakawa Sata Shrine." In ancient times, the shrine had priests appointed by the Ōmiya family, who conducted daily rituals. The shrine fell into disrepair during the Sengoku period but was restored in 1663 (Kanbun 3) with the efforts of Ōmiya priest Kawabe Masanaga.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.204-205 The shrine was rebuilt in January 1915 (Taisho 4),Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11 reconstructed in November 1956 (Showa 31), and repaired in June 1979 (Showa 54). == Festivals == Local leaders visit the shrine annually on January 17. The shrine holds Inari Festival (February 20), Tsukinami Festival (June 20 and December 20), and Niiname Festival (November 27) in the style of the Inner Shrine's pilgrimage rituals. == Flora == Camellias planted within the shrine grounds bloom in spring. == Access == ;Public Transport * About 500 meters northeast of JR Sangu Line's Tamaki Station. * Just south of the Kayamachi bus stop on Mie Kotsu buses. ;By Car * About 6 minutes (3.7 km) north from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 530 (Sunny Road), along Mie Prefectural Route 717. There is no parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0503.html 狭田国生神社（さたくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|狭田国生神社|version=77002057|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Satakunari Shrine (Q17225991)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|lt=Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|WD=Q17225991|1=Satakunari Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.490344|136.637711|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181118 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225991}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225991]] [[ja:狭田国生神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
